title: Choice and comparison
description: Practice your understanding of measures of quality of education

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering getting a degree in computer science and are trying to decide between two colleges: college A and college B. 

Here is how they compare:

Measure | Source | College A | College B
-|-|-|-
**Accreditation** | Regional accrediting body | Yes | Yes 
**Ranking** | U.S. News and World Report | $\#50$ | $\#100$
**Awards** | Faculty and research recognition | Several | Few
**Publications** | Journal and conference citations | Many | Some 
**Reviews** | Student and alumni feedback | Positive | Mixed 

**Based on the table above, which option scores higher in *academic reputation* for computer science?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: College A
College B

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering getting a degree in engineering and are trying to decide between two colleges: college A and college B. 

Here is how they compare:

  Measure | Source | College A | College B
-|-|-|-
**Accreditation** | Regional accrediting body | Yes | Yes 
**Ranking** | U.S. News and World Report | $\#80$ | $\#20$
**Awards** | Faculty and research recognition | Some | Numerous
**Publications** | Journal and conference citations | A few | Extensive 
**Reviews** | Student and alumni feedback | Mixed | Highly Positive 

**Based on the table above, which option scores higher in *academic reputation* for engineering?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: College A
College B

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering getting a degree in business administration and are trying to decide between two colleges: college A and college B.

Here is how they compare:

  Measure| Source | College A| College B
-|-|-|-
**Accreditation** | Regional accrediting body | Yes | Yes 
**Ranking** | U.S. News and World Report | $\#30$ | $\#75$
**Awards** | Faculty and research recognition | Multiple | A few
**Publications** | Journal and conference citations | Numerous | Limited 
**Reviews** | Student and alumni feedback | Mostly Positive | Neutral 

**Based on the table above, which option scores higher in *academic reputation* for business administration?**"


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: College A
College B

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the term *academic rigor*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how selective a school is when choosing students based on their grades and test scores
the percent of students who finish their program on time
how challenging the classes, teaching, and expectations are at a school
how well-known and respected a school is because of its successes, teachers, and students who graduate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the term *academic reputation*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how selective a school is when choosing students based on their grades and test scores
the percent of students who finish their program on time
how challenging the classes, teaching, and expectations are at a school
how well-known and respected a school is because of its successes, teachers, and students who graduate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the term *academic selectivity*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how selective a school is when choosing students based on their grades and test scores
the percent of students who finish their program on time
how challenging the classes, teaching, and expectations are at a school
how well-known and respected a school is because of its successes, teachers, and students who graduate

assessment_items.question_content:
When choosing a school to attend, some factors can help you determine the *quality* of education offered there. 

**Which factors help determine if a school is a good educational investment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: academic rigor (how hard the classes are)
the number of sports teams a school has
graduation rates (how many students finish on time)
opinions from students and people who graduated

assessment_items.question_content:
When choosing a school to attend, some factors can help you determine the *quality* of education offered there. 

**Which factors help determine if a school is a good educational investment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: academic reputation (how well-known and respected a school is)
school's ranking in its collegiate sports
accreditation (official approval from a group checking a school's quality)
selectivity (how picky a school is when choosing students)

assessment_items.question_content:
When choosing a school to attend, some factors can help you determine the *quality* of education offered there. 

**Which factors help determine if a school is a good educational investment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: academic rigor (how challenging the classes are)
number of scholarships available
graduation rates (how many students finish on time)
accreditation (official approval from a group checking a school's quality)

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many school choices out there. When looking at a for-profit school, it is important to look a little more carefully than if you were looking at a public school.

**Why should you be more careful when looking into for-profit schools?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They might only care about money and not strong teaching.
They could have too many fun activities and parties.
They may have too many students in each class.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many school choices out there. When looking at a for-profit school, it is important to look a little more carefully than if you were looking at a public school.

**What should you watch out for when considering a for-profit school?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They might not provide a good quality education because they focus more on making money.
The school might not offer enough elective classes.
The school might have too many students in one classroom.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many school choices out there. When looking at a for-profit school, it is important to look a little more carefully than if you were looking at a public school.

**What might be an issue with some for-profit schools?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They might focus more on making money than on the quality of education.
Their classes might be too hard for anyone to pass.
The school might have too many students in one classroom.
